mechanism_narrative: A consistent increase in industrial production (INDPRO) signals strengthening real economic activity, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month correlation between INDPRO growth and forward 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.1, or the signal produces negative returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0%, the labor market becomes very tight, raising the risk of wage inflation and subsequent monetary tightening by the Fed, which depresses equity valuations over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 60% of instances when UNRATE < 4.0% over any out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts (more than 10%) signals a sudden drop in consumer confidence and economic weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 10% from previous month
failure_condition: Signal produces positive forward 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances across walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) rise concurrently with consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL), it indicates demand-pull inflation that pressures the Fed to tighten, hurting equities over the next 20 trading days. The interactive effect of both rising together amplifies the negative signal.
failure_condition: The interaction term (smoothed product of RSAFS and CPIAUCSL growth rates) fails to achieve a significantly negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and decreases in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) reflect a robust labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When both PAYEMS is rising and UNRATE is falling, the forward 20-day return is negative in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation regime (CPIAUCSL yoy > 3%), an increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals aggressive tightening that compresses equity valuations and leads to negative forward returns over 20 trading days. The signal is conditional on the inflation threshold being exceeded.
failure_condition: In the high inflation regime, the correlation between FEDFUNDS changes and forward 20-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth (GDPC1) is strong but industrial production (INDPRO) is stagnant or contracting, the economy faces supply constraints that stoke inflationary pressures and erode profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 2% quarterly and INDPRO growth < 0% monthly
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances over any walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand, which drives corporate revenues and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The correlation between RSAFS growth and forward 20-day returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05) over any 5-year rolling window.
